title: Power harpoon launcher

{{Quote|Rogue Group, use your harpoons and tow cables. Go for the legs. It might be our only chance of stopping them.|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|<ref name="Episode 5">{{Film|V}}</ref>}}
The '''power harpoon launcher''', commonly referred to as a '''harpoon and tow cable''', was an industrial towing mechanism that was modified by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] to work as a weapon system.

==Design==
Manufactured by [[Ubrikkian Transports/Legends|Ubrikkian]], the power harpoon launcher consisted of a [[magnetic accelerator]], spools of [[high-tension cable]], and multiple [[fusion disk]]s. The launcher was usually mounted at the rear of the craft, and a swivel mount allowed for a degree of movement to allow the power harpoon to hit its target. An electronic sight was used to target the launcher, and this could be linked to a standard [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting computer]].<ref name="EGWT" />

The launcher's magnetic accelerator fired a fusion disk attached to a tow cable. The fusion disk was capable of attaching to any metallic surface, securely connecting to a target. Once the fusion disk was secured, the cable slack was taken in and locked in place. The launcher had a maximum range of two hundred [[Meter/Legends|meters]], although its optimum range was only one hundred meters. The power harpoon launcher could hold three spools of cable measuring two hundred meters long, and hold three fusion disks.<ref name="EGWT" />

Some models had an attached [[Fusioncutter/Legends|fusioncutter]] which could be used to cut the tow cable. The cable could then be left to drift free, or another fusion disk could be attached to it. This allowed several barges and skiffs to be linked together, or to allow cargo to be transferred to another two vehicle.<ref name="EGWT" />

Ubrikkian Transports manufactured the [[Mo/Dk power harpoon/Legends|Mo/Dk power harpoon]], which was commonly fitted to [[Snowspeeder/Legends|snowspeeders]] modified by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] [[Technician/Legends|technicians]].<ref name="CV">[[Star Wars: Complete Vehicles (2013)|''Star Wars: Complete Vehicles'']]</ref>

==History==
Power harpoons and tow cables were used across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as a means to tow cargo. They were attached to [[Spacetug/Legends|tugs]] and [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]] and then fixed to loaded [[Skiff/Legends|cargo skiffs]]. Because the skiffs were being towed by another vehicle that was providing the forward momentum, the skiffs could carry a lot more cargo since their [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlifts]] could focus solely on providing lift.<ref name="EGWT" />

[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] tactician [[Beryl Chiffonage]], in conjunction with [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], developed an offensive use for the power harpoon to combat [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]]. The tactic was used prominently during the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]].

Operated by the [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] onboard a [[Snowspeeder/Legends|snowspeeder]], the harpoon, tipped with a fusion disk, would be fired at the leg of an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] walker. The [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the snowspeeder would then make several rotations around the [[Walker/Legends|walker]] to wrap the [[flexisteel]] cable around its legs. The walker would then become ensnared and fall over, effectively neutralized. Harpoons and tow cables were able to bring down a few AT-ATs at [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] and were the Alliance's most effective method of dealing with the lethal Imperial walkers.

Besides the harpoon and tow cables themselves, there were also some types of weapons, such as the [[tow-cable bomb]], that were developed specifically for power harpoons in mind.<ref name="Rebel Strike III">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]''</ref>

It was employed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as a boarding weapon, employed by [[Assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttles]] to immobilize [[Starship/Legends|starships]] in preparation for a boarding action conducted by [[Zero-G assault stormtrooper]]s.<ref name="PD">{{WEGCite|book=Heir to the Empire Sourcebook|story=Pirate's Dilemma}}</ref>
